Wiring failure mode.question: Which is THE root cause of disable_usb_device not firing?
Wiring failure mode.follow_up: User wants outcome-driven framing, not root-cause picking. Reframed.

Wiring failure mode.question: What's the success criterion for Phase 38.2?
Re-scoping check — ROADMAP only covers PnP. Match your intent?
Which I/O interception layer for 38.2?
Wiring failure mode.answer: API hooking (initially) — then redirected to PnP+DACL after pitfalls explained

Wiring failure mode.question: After hearing API hooking pitfalls, do you still want it?
Wiring failure mode.answer: Asked clarifying question: 'do PnP + DACL provide realtime defending?'
options_presented: Switch to PnP + DACL, defer minifilter

Wiring failure mode.question: Does PnP + DACL meet your real-time-defense requirement?

locked_decisions.scope: Bulletproof PnP enforcement + Volume DACL deny-all as second OS-enforced layer. Two real-time, OS-enforced layers. No kernel driver, no DLL injection, no signing infrastructure beyond what already exists.
locked_decisions.researcher_focus: Enumerate every USB plug-in timing path (plug-before-agent, plug-during-startup-window, plug-while-running, server-disconnected, agent-restart-while-plugged) and prove enforcement covers all. Investigate the WR-01 USB_DEVICE↔VOLUME notification race already flagged in Phase 31 verification.
locked_decisions.primary_layer: PnP — CM_Disable_DevNode with CM_DISABLE_ABSOLUTE (already implemented in device_controller.rs:101)
locked_decisions.secondary_layer: DACL deny-all on volume root for Blocked tier. Extend the existing set_volume_readonly() pattern; reuse restore_volume_acl() for tier-change cleanup.
locked_decisions.audit_only_pipeline: The notify-based file watcher remains as observability ONLY. Not relied on for enforcement. May still emit BLOCK events for visibility but the ACTUAL enforcement happens at PnP + DACL layers before file I/O reaches the volume.

rejected_with_rationale.user_mode_API_hooking: Rejected. Concrete blockers: EDR conflict (Defender/CrowdStrike/SentinelOne detect third-party hooking as malware), PPL processes can't be injected, WoW64 split, AppInit_DLLs deprecated, trivially bypassed via cmd.exe/PowerShell, doesn't catch SYSTEM/kernel I/O, CET shadow-stack incompatibility on Win11. NOT real defense in depth — actually weaker than DACL.
rejected_with_rationale.windows_minifilter: Deferred to a future phase (likely v0.8.0 or later). Multi-week scope: WDK install, kernel debugging, test signing → EV signing for production, INF/CAT installer, BSOD risk during dev, IPC channel from driver to agent. Out of 38.2 scope per roadmap.

deferred_ideas: Windows minifilter driver (FltMgr) for kernel-level I/O interception — true defense-in-depth on top of PnP + DACL. Future phase candidate (v0.8.0+).
Per-process exception lists — minifilter would enable allowing specific processes through (e.g., admin tooling). Out of scope for 38.2.
canonical_refs: dlp-agent/src/usb_enforcer.rs (UsbEnforcer::check; line 152 known-USB-without-identity branch fires today)
dlp-agent/src/device_controller.rs (CM_Disable_DevNode and DACL machinery — both layers already implemented)
dlp-agent/src/detection/usb.rs (Win32 PnP arrival/removal handlers; pending_identity reconciliation; apply_tier_enforcement)
dlp-agent/src/service.rs (startup wiring of DEVICE_CONTROLLER, REGISTRY_CACHE, USB notifications)
